---
title: "project_testing"
output: html_document
date: "2025-05-03"
---


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
library(ggplot2)
library(xgboost)
library(randomForest)
library(glmnet)
library(e1071)
library(keras)
library(tidyr)
library(tensorflow)
library(lightgbm)
library(zoo)
```

```{r}
setwd("C:\\Users\\aryan\\Documents\\Rutgers\\Sem 2\\Finanicial Data Mining\\Project\\CMaps")
```

```{r}
file_ids <- c("FD001", "FD002", "FD003", "FD004")
file_types <- c("train", "test", "RUL")

# Column names for train and test data
columns <- c('unit_number', 'time_in_cycles', 'setting_1', 'setting_2', 'TRA', 'T2', 'T24', 'T30', 'T50',
             'P2', 'P15', 'P30', 'Nf', 'Nc', 'epr', 'Ps30', 'phi', 'NRf', 'NRc', 'BPR', 'farB', 'htBleed',
             'Nf_dmd', 'PCNfR_dmd', 'W31', 'W32')

# Step 1: Convert TXT to CSV (handle space issues)
for (file_type in file_types) {
  for (file_id in file_ids) {
    txt_file <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".txt")
    csv_file <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".csv")
    
    if (file.exists(txt_file)) {
      data <- read.table(txt_file, header = FALSE, sep = "", strip.white = TRUE)
      write.csv(data, csv_file, row.names = FALSE)
    }
  }
}
```

```{r}
clean_csv <- function(df) {
  df <- df[, colnames(df) != ""]
  return(df)
}
```

```{r}
data_list <- list()
for (file_type in file_types) {
  for (file_id in file_ids) {
    file_name <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id, ".csv")
    key <- paste0(file_type, "_", file_id)
    
    if (file.exists(file_name)) {
      df <- read.csv(file_name, header = TRUE)
      df <- clean_csv(df)
      
      # Add column names if train or test
      if (file_type %in% c("train", "test")) {
        colnames(df) <- columns
      }
      
      data_list[[key]] <- df
    }
  }
}

```

```{r}
prepare_train_data <- function(data, factor = 0) {
  fd_RUL <- data %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(max_time = max(time_in_cycles), .groups = 'drop')
  
  data <- merge(data, fd_RUL, by = "unit_number", all.x = TRUE)
  data$RUL <- data$max_time - data$time_in_cycles
  data <- subset(data, select = -max_time)
  data <- data[data$time_in_cycles > factor, ]
  
  return(data)
}

```

```{r}
prepared_train_data <- lapply(names(data_list), function(name) {
  if (startsWith(name, "train_")) {
    prepare_train_data(data_list[[name]])
  }
})

names(prepared_train_data) <- names(data_list)[startsWith(names(data_list), "train_")]

```

```{r}
# Plot RUL distribution
ggplot(prepared_train_data$train_FD001, aes(x=RUL)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=100, fill="blue", alpha=0.7) +
  labs(title="Distribution of Remaining Useful Life", x="RUL", y="Count")

# Visualize sensor readings over time for a few units
sample_units <- c(1, 2, 3)
sample_data <- prepared_train_data$train_FD001 %>% 
  filter(unit_number %in% sample_units)

# Plot key sensor readings vs. time
ggplot(sample_data, aes(x=time_in_cycles, y=T24, color=as.factor(unit_number))) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title="Sensor T24 Readings Over Time", x="Cycles", y="T24 Value", color="Unit") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
str(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)
summary(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)
```

```{r}
hist(prepared_train_data$train_FD001$RUL, 
     main="Distribution of RUL Values", 
     xlab="Remaining Useful Life", 
     col="lightblue", 
     breaks=30)
```

```{r}
plot_feature_distributions <- function(dataset, dataset_name) {
  numeric_cols <- sapply(dataset, is.numeric)
  numeric_data <- dataset[, numeric_cols]
  
  # Exclude unit_number, time_in_cycles, and RUL for sensor analysis
  sensor_data <- numeric_data[, !names(numeric_data) %in% c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL")]
  
  par(mfrow=c(3, 4))
  for(col in names(sensor_data)) {
    hist(sensor_data[[col]], 
         main=paste("Distribution of", col), 
         xlab=col, 
         col="lightblue")
  }
  par(mfrow=c(1, 1))
  title(main=paste("Feature Distributions for", dataset_name), outer=TRUE)
}

plot_feature_distributions(prepared_train_data$train_FD001, "FD001")
```

```{r}
analyze_unit_degradation <- function(dataset, unit_ids, features) {
  for(unit in unit_ids) {
    unit_data <- dataset[dataset$unit_number == unit, ]
    
    plot_data <- unit_data[, c("time_in_cycles", features)]
    plot_data_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(plot_data, 
                                      cols = all_of(features),
                                      names_to = "feature", 
                                      values_to = "value")
    
    ggplot(plot_data_long, aes(x = time_in_cycles, y = value, color = feature)) +
      geom_line() +
      facet_wrap(~ feature, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
      labs(title = paste("Sensor Values Over Time for Unit", unit),
           x = "Time (cycles)",
           y = "Sensor Value") +
      theme_minimal()
  }
}

# Analyze the first 3 units with all remaining sensors
important_features <- setdiff(names(prepared_train_data$train_FD001), 
                            c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL"))
analyze_unit_degradation(prepared_train_data$train_FD001, 1:3, important_features)
```

```{r}
# Define all sensor features excluding metadata columns
define_sensor_features <- function(dataset) {
  # Get all numeric columns except unit_number, time_in_cycles, RUL
  exclude_cols <- c('unit_number', 'time_in_cycles', 'RUL', 'max_time')
  all_cols <- names(dataset)
  sensor_cols <- setdiff(all_cols, exclude_cols)
  
  # Filter to only include numeric columns
  numeric_sensor_cols <- sensor_cols[sapply(dataset[, sensor_cols, drop=FALSE], is.numeric)]
  
  return(numeric_sensor_cols)
}

# Apply this function to properly define sensor_features
sensor_features <- define_sensor_features(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)

feature_stats_by_unit <- function(dataset, features) {
  dataset %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    summarize(across(all_of(features), 
                    list(mean = mean, 
                         sd = sd, 
                         trend = ~cor(.x, time_in_cycles, use = "complete.obs"))))
}

non_constant_features <- sensor_features[sapply(sensor_features, function(f) sd(prepared_train_data$train_FD001[[f]]) > 0)]

unit_stats <- feature_stats_by_unit(prepared_train_data$train_FD001, non_constant_features)
head(unit_stats)
```

```{r}
for (feature in sensor_features) {  
  plot_data <- prepared_train_data$train_FD001[, c(feature, "RUL")]  
  plot_data <- na.omit(plot_data)  # Remove NA rows  
  plot_data <- plot_data[is.finite(plot_data[[feature]]) & is.finite(plot_data$RUL), ]  # Remove infinities  
 
  if (sd(plot_data[[feature]]) > 0) {  # Ensure feature varies
    plot(plot_data[[feature]], plot_data$RUL,  
         main=paste("RUL vs", feature),  
         xlab=feature, ylab="RUL",  
         col="blue", pch=19, cex=0.5)  
    abline(lm(RUL ~ ., data=plot_data), col="red")  
  } else {  
    message(paste("Skipping", feature, "due to zero variance"))  
  }  
}
```

```{r}
compare_datasets <- function() {
  results <- data.frame()
  for(ds_name in names(prepared_train_data)) {
    ds <- prepared_train_data[[ds_name]]
    
    # Basic metrics
    n_units <- length(unique(ds$unit_number))
    mean_rul <- mean(ds$RUL)
    min_rul <- min(ds$RUL)
    max_rul <- max(ds$RUL)
    
    # Add to results
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Dataset = ds_name,
      Units = n_units,
      Mean_RUL = mean_rul,
      Min_RUL = min_rul,
      Max_RUL = max_rul
    ))
  }
  return(results)
}

dataset_comparison <- compare_datasets()
print(dataset_comparison)
```

```{r}
compute_rolling_stats <- function(dataset, window_size = 10) {
  result_data <- dataset %>%
    group_by(unit_number) %>%
    arrange(unit_number, time_in_cycles) %>%
    mutate(
      # Rolling mean for each sensor
      across(all_of(sensor_features), 
             list(rolling_mean = ~zoo::rollmean(.x, k = window_size, fill = NA, align = "right")),
             .names = "{.col}_rolling_mean"),
      # Rolling standard deviation
      across(all_of(sensor_features), 
             list(rolling_sd = ~zoo::rollapply(.x, width = window_size, FUN = sd, fill = NA, align = "right")),
             .names = "{.col}_rolling_sd"),
      # Rate of change (slope over window)
      across(all_of(sensor_features), 
             list(slope = ~zoo::rollapply(.x, width = window_size, 
                                         FUN = function(x) {
                                           if(length(unique(x)) == 1) return(0)
                                           return(coef(lm(x ~ seq_along(x)))[2])
                                         }, 
                                         fill = NA, align = "right")),
             .names = "{.col}_slope")
    )
  
  return(result_data)
}

# Apply to first dataset with a window of 10 cycles
fd001_with_rolling <- compute_rolling_stats(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)
```

```{r}
sample_unit <- 1
sample_feature <- sensor_features[1]

unit_data <- fd001_with_rolling[fd001_with_rolling$unit_number == sample_unit, ]
plot(unit_data$time_in_cycles, unit_data[[sample_feature]], 
     type="l", col="blue", 
     main=paste("Original vs Rolling Features for Unit", sample_unit),
     xlab="Cycles", ylab="Value")
lines(unit_data$time_in_cycles, unit_data[[paste0(sample_feature, "_rolling_mean")]], col="red", lwd=2)
legend("topright", legend=c("Original", "Rolling Mean"), 
       col=c("blue", "red"), lty=1)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

# Take a clean subset for modeling
model_data <- na.omit(fd001_with_rolling)
# Select some columns to avoid memory issues
selected_cols <- c(
  "RUL", 
  sensor_features,
  paste0(sensor_features, "_rolling_mean"),
  paste0(sensor_features, "_slope")
)
model_data <- model_data[, selected_cols]

# Train a basic random forest to get feature importance
rf_model <- randomForest(RUL ~ ., data=model_data, ntree=50, importance=TRUE)
feature_importance <- importance(rf_model)
feature_importance <- data.frame(
  Feature = rownames(feature_importance),
  Importance = feature_importance[, "%IncMSE"]
)
feature_importance <- feature_importance[order(feature_importance$Importance, decreasing=TRUE), ]

# Visualize top 15 most important features
ggplot(head(feature_importance, 15), aes(x=reorder(Feature, Importance), y=Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title="Top 15 Features by Importance",
       x="Feature",
       y="Importance (%IncMSE)") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
prepare_model_data <- function(dataset, features_to_use) {
  # Make sure features_to_use exist in the dataset
  features_to_use <- intersect(features_to_use, colnames(dataset))
  
  if (length(features_to_use) == 0) {
    stop("No valid features provided for modeling")
  }
  
  # Ensure RUL column exists
  if (!"RUL" %in% colnames(dataset)) {
    stop("RUL column not found in dataset")
  }
  
  # Include unit_number, time_in_cycles, RUL and the specified features
  cols_to_use <- unique(c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles", "RUL", features_to_use))
  cols_to_use <- intersect(cols_to_use, colnames(dataset))  # Ensure all columns exist
  
  # Remove NA values
  model_data <- na.omit(dataset[, cols_to_use])
  
  # Check if we have enough data
  if (nrow(model_data) < 10) {
    stop("Not enough data after removing NA values")
  }
  
  # Split by units to avoid data leakage
  all_units <- unique(model_data$unit_number)
  
  if (length(all_units) < 2) {
    stop("Need at least 2 units for train/validation split")
  }
  
  set.seed(123)
  train_size <- max(1, floor(0.7 * length(all_units)))
  train_units <- sample(all_units, size = train_size)
  val_units <- setdiff(all_units, train_units)
  
  # Create training and validation sets
  train_data <- model_data[model_data$unit_number %in% train_units, ]
  val_data <- model_data[model_data$unit_number %in% val_units, ]
  
  # If either set is empty, adjust the split
  if (nrow(train_data) == 0 || nrow(val_data) == 0) {
    # Just do a random split instead
    set.seed(123)
    train_idx <- sample(1:nrow(model_data), size = floor(0.7 * nrow(model_data)))
    train_data <- model_data[train_idx, ]
    val_data <- model_data[-train_idx, ]
  }
  
  # Get features only (no unit_number or time_in_cycles) for modeling
  train_data_x <- train_data[, !(names(train_data) %in% c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles"))]
  val_data_x <- val_data[, !(names(val_data) %in% c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles"))]
  
  return(list(
    train_data = train_data,
    val_data = val_data,
    train_data_x = train_data_x,
    val_data_x = val_data_x
  ))
}
```

```{r}
get_important_features <- function(data, top_n = 20) {
  set.seed(123)
  
  # Remove non-numeric columns and those that shouldn't be used as features
  cols_to_exclude <- c("unit_number", "time_in_cycles")
  feature_cols <- setdiff(names(data), cols_to_exclude)
  
  # Make sure we have the RUL column
  if (!"RUL" %in% names(data)) {
    stop("RUL column not found in the dataset")
  }
  
  # Keep only numeric columns for modeling
  is_numeric <- sapply(data[, feature_cols, drop=FALSE], is.numeric)
  feature_cols <- feature_cols[is_numeric]
  
  # Remove any columns with all NAs or constant values
  valid_cols <- c()
  for (col in feature_cols) {
    if (!all(is.na(data[[col]])) && sd(data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE) > 0) {
      valid_cols <- c(valid_cols, col)
    }
  }
  
  # If we don't have enough valid columns, return what we have
  if (length(valid_cols) == 0) {
    message("No valid feature columns found")
    return(list(
      top_features = character(0),
      importance_df = data.frame(Feature = character(0), Importance = numeric(0))
    ))
  }
  
  # Prepare formula for randomForest
  formula_str <- paste("RUL ~", paste(valid_cols, collapse = " + "))
  rf_formula <- as.formula(formula_str)
  
  # Run random forest with error handling
  tryCatch({
    rf_model <- randomForest(rf_formula, data = data, 
                           ntree = 50, importance = TRUE)
    
    feature_importance <- importance(rf_model)
    feature_importance_df <- data.frame(
      Feature = rownames(feature_importance),
      Importance = feature_importance[, "%IncMSE"]
    )
    feature_importance_df <- feature_importance_df[order(feature_importance_df$Importance, decreasing = TRUE), ]
    
    # Get top N features or all if fewer than top_n
    n_to_select <- min(top_n, nrow(feature_importance_df))
    top_features <- as.character(head(feature_importance_df$Feature, n_to_select))
    
    return(list(
      top_features = top_features,
      importance_df = feature_importance_df
    ))
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Error in random forest feature selection:", e$message))
    # Return all valid columns if RF fails
    return(list(
      top_features = valid_cols,
      importance_df = data.frame(Feature = valid_cols, Importance = rep(1, length(valid_cols)))
    ))
  })
}
```

```{r}
calculate_metrics <- function(actual, predicted) {
  rmse <- sqrt(mean((actual - predicted)^2))
  mae <- mean(abs(actual - predicted))
  r_squared <- 1 - (sum((actual - predicted)^2) / sum((actual - mean(actual))^2))
  
  return(list(
    RMSE = rmse,
    MAE = mae,
    R_squared = r_squared
  ))
}
```

```{r}
train_evaluate_model <- function(train_data, val_data, model_type, params = NULL) {
  x_train <- train_data[, !(names(train_data) %in% c("RUL"))]
  y_train <- train_data$RUL
  
  x_val <- val_data[, !(names(val_data) %in% c("RUL"))]
  y_val <- val_data$RUL
  
  start_time <- Sys.time()
  
  # Train model based on type
  if (model_type == "rf") {
    ntrees <- ifelse(is.null(params$ntrees), 500, params$ntrees)
    mtry <- ifelse(is.null(params$mtry), floor(sqrt(ncol(x_train))), params$mtry)
    
    model <- randomForest(x = x_train, y = y_train, 
                         ntree = ntrees, mtry = mtry, 
                         importance = TRUE)
    
    train_pred <- predict(model, x_train)
    val_pred <- predict(model, x_val)
    
  } else if (model_type == "xgb") {
    # Convert data to xgb format
    dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(x_train), label = y_train)
    dval <- xgb.DMatrix(data = as.matrix(x_val), label = y_val)
    
    # Default params
    xgb_params <- list(
      objective = "reg:squarederror",
      eta = ifelse(is.null(params$eta), 0.1, params$eta),
      max_depth = ifelse(is.null(params$max_depth), 6, params$max_depth),
      subsample = ifelse(is.null(params$subsample), 0.8, params$subsample),
      colsample_bytree = ifelse(is.null(params$colsample_bytree), 0.8, params$colsample_bytree)
    )
    
    watchlist <- list(train = dtrain, val = dval)
    
    model <- xgb.train(
      params = xgb_params,
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = ifelse(is.null(params$nrounds), 100, params$nrounds),
      watchlist = watchlist,
      early_stopping_rounds = 10,
      verbose = 0
    )
    
    train_pred <- predict(model, dtrain)
    val_pred <- predict(model, dval)
    
  } else if (model_type == "elastic_net") {
    # Normalize data
    x_train_matrix <- as.matrix(x_train)
    x_val_matrix <- as.matrix(x_val)
    
    alpha <- ifelse(is.null(params$alpha), 0.5, params$alpha)
    
    # Cross-validation to find optimal lambda
    cv_model <- cv.glmnet(x_train_matrix, y_train, alpha = alpha)
    
    # Use lambda.min or lambda.1se
    lambda <- cv_model$lambda.min
    
    model <- glmnet(x_train_matrix, y_train, alpha = alpha, lambda = lambda)
    
    train_pred <- predict(model, x_train_matrix, s = lambda)
    val_pred <- predict(model, x_val_matrix, s = lambda)
    
  } else if (model_type == "lightgbm") {
    # Convert data to lgb format
    dtrain <- lgb.Dataset(data = as.matrix(x_train), label = y_train)
    
    # Default params
    lgb_params <- list(
      objective = "regression",
      metric = "rmse",
      learning_rate = ifelse(is.null(params$learning_rate), 0.1, params$learning_rate),
      num_leaves = ifelse(is.null(params$num_leaves), 31, params$num_leaves),
      feature_fraction = ifelse(is.null(params$feature_fraction), 0.8, params$feature_fraction)
    )
    
    model <- lgb.train(
      params = lgb_params,
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = ifelse(is.null(params$nrounds), 100, params$nrounds),
      verbose = -1
    )
    
    train_pred <- predict(model, as.matrix(x_train))
    val_pred <- predict(model, as.matrix(x_val))
  }
  
  end_time <- Sys.time()
  training_time <- difftime(end_time, start_time, units = "secs")
  
  # Calculate metrics
  train_metrics <- calculate_metrics(y_train, train_pred)
  val_metrics <- calculate_metrics(y_val, val_pred)
  
  return(list(
    model = model,
    training_time = training_time,
    train_metrics = train_metrics,
    val_metrics = val_metrics,
    train_pred = train_pred,
    val_pred = val_pred,
    actual_train = y_train,
    actual_val = y_val
  ))
}
```

```{r}
run_model_assessment <- function() {
  results_table <- data.frame()
  
  for (ds_name in names(prepared_train_data)) {
    # Get dataset with engineered features
    dataset <- compute_rolling_stats(prepared_train_data[[ds_name]])
    
    # Remove NA values before feature selection
    dataset <- na.omit(dataset)
    
    # Get important features
    feature_results <- get_important_features(dataset, top_n = 20)
    top_features <- feature_results$top_features
    
    # Make sure we have the required columns in the dataset
    if (!all(top_features %in% colnames(dataset))) {
      message(paste("Some top features are missing in", ds_name, "- using available features only"))
      top_features <- top_features[top_features %in% colnames(dataset)]
    }
    
    # Skip if no features are available
    if (length(top_features) == 0) {
      message(paste("No valid features for", ds_name, "- skipping this dataset"))
      next
    }
    
    # Prepare data for modeling
    model_data <- prepare_model_data(dataset, top_features)
    
    # Check if we have enough data
    if (nrow(model_data$train_data_x) < 10 || nrow(model_data$val_data_x) < 10) {
      message(paste("Not enough data for", ds_name, "after preparation - skipping this dataset"))
      next
    }
    
    # Train and evaluate different models
    models_to_test <- list(
      rf = list(ntrees = 200, mtry = min(5, length(top_features)-1)), # Ensure mtry is not larger than available features
      xgb = list(eta = 0.05, max_depth = 5, nrounds = 200),
      elastic_net = list(alpha = 0.5),
      lightgbm = list(learning_rate = 0.05, num_leaves = 31, nrounds = 200)
    )
    
    for (model_name in names(models_to_test)) {
      tryCatch({
        results <- train_evaluate_model(
          model_data$train_data_x, 
          model_data$val_data_x, 
          model_type = model_name,
          params = models_to_test[[model_name]]
        )
        
        # Add to results table
        results_table <- rbind(results_table, data.frame(
          Dataset = ds_name,
          Model = model_name,
          RMSE_Train = results$train_metrics$RMSE,
          RMSE_Val = results$val_metrics$RMSE,
          MAE_Train = results$train_metrics$MAE,
          MAE_Val = results$val_metrics$MAE,
          R2_Train = results$train_metrics$R_squared,
          R2_Val = results$val_metrics$R_squared,
          Training_Time_Sec = as.numeric(results$training_time)
        ))
        
        # Visualize predictions vs actual for validation set
        val_results <- data.frame(
          Actual = results$actual_val,
          Predicted = results$val_pred
        )
        
        plot_title <- paste("Actual vs Predicted RUL -", ds_name, "-", model_name)
        
        p <- ggplot(val_results, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
          geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = "blue") +
          geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
          labs(title = plot_title,
               x = "Actual RUL",
               y = "Predicted RUL") +
          theme_minimal() +
          annotate("text", x = min(val_results$Actual), y = max(val_results$Predicted),
                  label = paste("RMSE:", round(results$val_metrics$RMSE, 2),
                               "\nMAE:", round(results$val_metrics$MAE, 2),
                               "\nR²:", round(results$val_metrics$R_squared, 3)),
                  hjust = 0, vjust = 1)
        
        print(p)
      }, error = function(e) {
        message(paste("Error in", model_name, "for", ds_name, ":", e$message))
      })
    }
  }
  
  return(results_table)
}

```

```{r}
# After you've prepared your train data:

# 1. Define sensor features dynamically based on your data
sensor_features <- define_sensor_features(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)
print("Identified sensor features:")
print(sensor_features)

# 2. First compute rolling statistics for one dataset to test
fd001_with_rolling <- compute_rolling_stats(prepared_train_data$train_FD001)
print(paste("Original columns:", ncol(prepared_train_data$train_FD001), 
           "After rolling stats:", ncol(fd001_with_rolling)))

# 3. Run a test on just one model to make sure everything works
test_dataset <- na.omit(fd001_with_rolling)
test_features <- get_important_features(test_dataset, top_n = 10)
print("Top 10 features:")
print(test_features$top_features)

# 4. Test prepare_model_data function
test_model_data <- prepare_model_data(test_dataset, test_features$top_features)
print(paste("Training samples:", nrow(test_model_data$train_data_x),
           "Validation samples:", nrow(test_model_data$val_data_x)))

# 5. Test one model
test_results <- train_evaluate_model(
  test_model_data$train_data_x,
  test_model_data$val_data_x,
  model_type = "rf",
  params = list(ntrees = 50, mtry = 3)
)
print("Test model metrics:")
print(test_results$val_metrics)

# 6. Only if all the above works properly, run the full assessment
model_results <- run_model_assessment()

# 7. Visualize results
ggplot(model_results, aes(x = Model, y = RMSE_Val, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Model Performance Comparison",
       x = "Model Type",
       y = "Validation RMSE") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}
model_results <- run_model_assessment()

# Visualize model comparison
ggplot(model_results, aes(x = Model, y = RMSE_Val, fill = Dataset)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Model Performance Comparison",
       x = "Model Type",
       y = "Validation RMSE") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```


```{r}

```


```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```


```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```


```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```

```{r}

```
